growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron's March 18 results confirmed a much stronger AI memory cycle than the market expected, with record Q2 FY26 revenue of $23.86 billion, Q3 guidance for $33.5 billion, and HBM4 volume shipments starting for NVIDIA Vera Rubin systems.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron is a high-quality overlap winner in the AI memory cycle: record Q2 FY2026 results, a record Q3 guide, and HBM4 volume shipments for NVIDIA Vera Rubin support a still-cheap forward multiple after a 30% drawdown from the high.
Core ads are still growing fast and funding an AI infrastructure moat, while META screens reasonably valued on a 15.8x forward P/E despite an April 29 earnings catalyst.

ServiceNow is a high-quality software compounder trading about 50% below its 52-week high, with 20%+ growth, nearly $5.0B of FCF, above-consensus 2026 subscription guidance, and a fresh CEO open-market buy supporting a rebound if April earnings validate AI demand.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Microsoft's March 9 launch of the Frontier Suite creates a higher-value AI bundle around Copilot, Agent 365 and security, and its March 26 adoption update showed 24 billion audited Copilot interactions, 26 million GitHub Copilot users and broad enterprise proof of monetization.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft is a high-quality AI compounder with proven Copilot monetization, strong cash generation, and a near-term earnings catalyst, though heavy AI infrastructure spend keeps it below STRONG BUY.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Cheddar Flow highlighted roughly $3 million of bullish upside call premium in NVDA around March 20, 2026 while the same market snapshot also showed about $1.90 billion of dark-pool premium at $178.56, which is not definitive but is large enough to flag as institutional positioning ahead of AI-demand headlines.
growth_picks.analyst_take: NVDA remains a high-quality AI infrastructure leader with record fiscal 2026 results and a credible May 2026 earnings catalyst, but I would stop short of STRONG BUY because China exposure and elevated expectations still cap near-term upside certainty.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Salesforce announced on March 11, 2026 a $25B accelerated share repurchase as the first tranche of its new authorization to buy back up to $50B, which is an unusually aggressive capital-return signal from a mega-cap after activist pressure and margin expansion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce looks like a mispriced quality software compounder: forward valuation is reasonable, free cash flow is huge, Agentforce/Data 360 adoption is accelerating, and management just launched a historic $25B ASR while directors bought stock in March.

CRSR is a cheap, cash-generative gaming-hardware name with insider buying and a fresh $50 million buyback, but the near-term revenue guide is still soft because Gaming Components and Systems remains constrained.

Coupang looks like a contrarian rebound buy near a major insider accumulation zone as core commerce margins and Taiwan growth offset a temporary data-breach hit, but the legal and regulatory overhang caps conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NVIDIA's March 11 investment and partnership with Nebius to scale full-stack AI cloud infrastructure materially de-risks customer acquisition, while Nebius had already disclosed March ARR of at least $220 million and an end-2026 capacity target of 800MW to 1GW.
growth_picks.analyst_take: NVIDIA's $2 billion strategic investment and Meta's up-to-$27 billion infrastructure agreement validate Nebius's AI-cloud demand story, but the stock still carries meaningful financing, dilution, and execution risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Accenture is showing real enterprise AI monetization rather than slideware, with record $22.1 billion bookings in its March 19, 2026 quarter and a new Databricks-focused business group backed by 25,000 trained professionals to move clients from pilots into scaled AI applications and agents.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Accenture is a beaten-down, cash-generative services leader showing real enterprise AI monetization, and its mid-teens earnings multiple already discounts federal and consulting softness.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Nikesh Arora bought 68,085 PANW shares in open-market trades on March 27, 2026 at roughly $146.87-$147.48 for about $10.0 million, a rare sizable CEO cash purchase in a large-cap software name.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Rare CEO open-market buying into a 28% drawdown, combined with 33% Next-Generation Security ARR growth and fresh AI security launches, supports a quality buy despite a still-rich multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista's March 12 XPO liquid-cooled optics MSA targets 204.8 Tbps per rack unit, a 4x density jump versus 1600G-OSFP optics, which increases its content opportunity as AI racks get hotter and denser.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Arista is a high-quality AI and cloud networking compounder with real near-term demand, but the stock is still priced for execution and hyperscaler spending must stay firm.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Fivespan Partners and CIO Dylan Haggart filed a new Schedule 13D on March 12, 2026 after buying 3,013,943 shares, or 5.1% of BlackLine, for about $138.6M and explicitly said the stock was undervalued and that they expect discussions with management and the board.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Activist pressure, a larger buyback, and a May 2026 earnings catalyst create rerating potential for BlackLine, though the case is capped by only modest growth and leadership-transition risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Corcept flipped from regulatory skepticism to a fresh FDA approval on March 25, creating a sharp narrative reset with commercial upside and renewed momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FDA approval for relacorilant in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer resets the story and adds a new growth engine, while insider buying and a 65% drawdown improve the setup despite remaining legal and execution risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Sysco became interesting after the market punished its Restaurant Depot deal, because the acquisition gives it immediate scale in a faster-growing and higher-margin cash-and-carry channel.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Sysco's core business is inflecting ahead of April 28 earnings, and the post-deal selloff looks overdone if management delivers on reiterated FY26 guidance and makes the Restaurant Depot acquisition accretive as promised.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Robinhood's board authorized a new $1.5B share repurchase program in late March 2026, adding more than $1.1B of fresh capacity and signaling management is willing to shrink share count aggressively while the business is producing excess capital.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Robinhood looks like a high-quality but still volatile fintech reset: the stock is down 55.4% from its 52-week high while the business is still growing customers, assets, and crypto volumes, and management just reloaded the buyback.

HRB is a cash-generative tax-prep franchise trading at roughly 6x forward EPS with May tax-season results and ongoing buybacks as catalysts, though the highly seasonal funding profile limits conviction.

Profitability and free-cash-flow improved materially in 2025 and management added a $500M buyback, but Snap is still heavily tied to cyclical ad spend and the 2026 Specs launch is not enough on its own to justify a buy today.

Cheap on forward earnings with a planned tax-free split, but weak automotive margins, tariff and separation execution risk, and no notable open-market insider buying keep Genuine Parts in wait-and-see mode.

McCormick still has a resilient core flavor franchise and reaffirmed 2026 outlook, but the newly announced Unilever Foods transaction adds major leverage and integration risk that keeps the risk/reward from clearing a fresh-buy bar.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Geopolitical risk around Hormuz and structurally tighter European gas balances raise the value of flexible US LNG supply, and Cheniere remains the cleanest large-cap way to monetize higher Atlantic Basin security demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheniere remains a high-quality LNG cash-flow compounder with visible 2026 train-completion and buyback catalysts, but the stock is already near highs with limited valuation upside and no supportive insider buying.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Replimune is a classic binary-event setup with an April 10, 2026 FDA action date that could materially revalue the company if RP1 is approved.
growth_picks.analyst_take: April 10, 2026 PDUFA creates real upside and squeeze optionality, but the prior CRL challenged RP1's efficacy package at the study-design level and the current setup does not offer enough risk/reward for a fresh BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Eaton looks like a sector-rotation winner as data-center power demand keeps pushing orders and backlog higher while the company still prints industrial-grade margins.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality electrification and AI power-infrastructure winner, but the stock still looks priced for execution with limited valuation cushion.

catalysts_general: Discovery: GE Vernova sits in the middle of the power-generation and grid buildout needed for AI, with demand now broadening beyond just one-off data-center projects.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GE Vernova has real power and grid demand momentum with a large backlog and strong cash generation, but the stock already prices in much of that upside at a rich multiple near its highs.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Lululemon is still highly profitable but nearly 60% below its 52-week high after its March 17 guide-down, and the next catalyst is the roughly June 3, 2026 Q1 report where management has to show North America product and inventory fixes are working; bear case is that tariff pressure and U.S. demand softness linger longer than management expects.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap for a brand of this quality, but the thesis still depends on the early-June quarter proving North America demand, inventory discipline, and margin repair are actually stabilizing.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Chair and CEO Robert Katz bought roughly $4.9 million of stock in the open market, a rare high-conviction insider signal after a long slide in the shares.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Rare March insider buying and durable pass economics support the floor, but weather-driven guidance cuts, negative visitation trends, and legal/leverage risks keep Vail Resorts short of a fresh buy here.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Oracle's March 10 quarter showed AI cloud demand converting into backlog and revenue, with IaaS up 84%, multicloud database revenue up 531%, and remaining performance obligations reaching $553 billion.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI cloud demand is real and accelerating, but Oracle still has to prove that its massive backlog converts into cash without further balance-sheet strain or equity dilution.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Axsome has a clean near-term binary catalyst with an April 30 FDA decision on AXS-05 for Alzheimer's agitation layered on top of strong commercial momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Strong commercial growth and an April 30 AXS-05 FDA catalyst keep upside alive, but with shares near 52-week highs, negative free cash flow, mixed Alzheimer’s agitation trial history, and no recent open-market insider buying, the setup is no longer cheap enough for a fresh BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO's March 27 estimate of roughly $145 billion in alliance munitions demand, combined with March 19 emergency Middle East radar and missile sales, points to incremental demand for RTX air-defense radars, AMRAAMs, NASAMS content and reloads before consensus fully resets.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality defense and aerospace franchise with real 2026 demand catalysts, but the stock is already near highs and not cheap enough to underwrite unresolved Pratt & Whitney and disclosure risks as a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Fresh THAAD-linked emergency sales to the Gulf and the Pentagon's March 25 push to increase Precision Strike Missile output reinforce a multi-theater missile demand cycle that still looks underappreciated relative to Lockheed's missile backlog optionality.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Missile-defense and F-35 demand remain durable, but with shares near prior highs and only modest upside versus fair value, Lockheed looks like a quality hold rather than a fresh value entry.

catalysts_general: Discovery: IFF logged a credible March insider cluster: director Paul Fribourg bought 14,200 shares for about $994,994 directly while Continental Grain, an entity under his control, bought 142,000 shares for about $9,958,460 on March 12, and CEO Erik Fyrwald followed with 14,260 shares for about $995,204 on March 12-13, a roughly $11.95 million wave of open-market buying.
growth_picks.analyst_take: IFF has credible insider support and tangible portfolio-simplification catalysts, but declining Food Ingredients sales, a large 2025 goodwill impairment, and still-soft reported growth keep it below a fresh-buy threshold ahead of May earnings.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Starboard Value surfaced as a significant CarMax stockholder on March 11, 2026 with 6.20M shares held directly plus swap exposure to 2.10M more shares, and Reuters valued the activist stake at roughly $350M as Starboard pushed board and operating changes.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Starboard's activist push and the April 14, 2026 earnings report could reset sentiment, but weak retail volumes, thin margins, and rising auto-finance credit stress keep CarMax in prove-it mode.

catalysts_general: Discovery: HIMS screens as a live squeeze setup with 40.22% short float while fundamentals are still improving fast, including 59.00% TTM sales growth, 28.41% Q/Q sales growth, and 40.45% EPS growth expected next year, reinforced by the March 9 Novo Nordisk collaboration and lawsuit dismissal.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fast revenue growth, a revived Novo Nordisk relationship, and heavy short interest keep upside alive, but active regulatory and securities overhangs make the risk/reward too mixed for a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Rexford caught attention because multiple executives bought shares after a sharp REIT selloff, a cleaner insider signal than isolated option-linked trades.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Late-February insider buying and a fresh $500 million repurchase authorization support the downside, but 2026 same-property NOI and occupancy are guided lower, so REXR still needs an April earnings proof point before it earns a BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Centene is a profitable managed-care name that appears washed out after a March investor-day reset, creating a potential rebound setup if 2026 margin and membership assumptions prove conservative.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Centene is statistically cheap and still generating strong cash flow, but March 2026 ACA membership attrition and unresolved marketplace margin risk keep the setup in prove-it territory ahead of late-April earnings.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Entergy is an underappreciated second-order AI beneficiary after signing a new March 27, 2026 agreement with Meta that adds another $2 billion of customer savings and extends infrastructure support for one of the largest U.S. AI data center campuses in Louisiana.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Entergy has credible AI-driven load-growth upside through the Meta and broader data-center buildout, but at roughly its 52-week high with limited sell-side upside, negative FCF, and material regulatory/capex risk, the stock is not attractive enough to buy here.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Etsy is profitable, roughly 38% below its 52-week high and less than 19% above its low, with April 29, 2026 earnings and the expected Q2 2026 close of the $1.2 billion Depop sale giving management a near-term chance to prove the core marketplace can stabilize and buybacks can be funded from proceeds; bear case is that GMS weakness persists and the asset sale merely masks core stagnation.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap on forward earnings and cash flow with April earnings and the Q2 Depop sale as catalysts, but the core Etsy marketplace is only barely stabilizing and recent insider filings do not show open-market buying.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Macy's returned to annual comparable-sales growth and topped guidance, yet the stock still trades with legacy-retail pessimism and elevated short interest.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Macy's is cheap and cash-generative, but management's cautious 2026 guide and tariff-exposed discretionary demand leave limited upside until the May earnings print proves the turnaround.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Co-founder and CEO Jeff Green made an unscheduled open-market purchase of 6.0M shares for about $148.1M between March 2-4, 2026, an unusually large founder buy after a sharp drawdown that reads as high-conviction dip buying.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Founder buying and a cheapened forward multiple make TTD interesting, but the Publicis transparency dispute and soft Q1 ad verticals leave too much unresolved to upgrade before the next earnings report.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Better screens as a high-beta insider signal because the CEO and insider investor Framework both kept buying stock into late March weakness.
growth_picks.analyst_take: BETR has credible near-term catalysts from Tinman platform ramp and heavy March insider buying, but persistent losses, extreme leverage, and mortgage-rate sensitivity keep the risk/reward below a clean buy threshold.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Duolingo remains profitable but is more than 80% below its 52-week high after a violent guidance reset, with the next prove-it catalyst on April 30, 2026 earnings; bear case is that multiple compression can continue if growth decelerates again.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Duolingo is still a high-quality, cash-generative education platform, but the 2026 pivot toward user growth over monetization leaves bookings and margin pressure unresolved after the February guidance reset.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Avoid candidate: BigBear.ai's March 2, 2026 report exposed the gap between AI narrative and fundamentals, with Q4 revenue down 38% year over year, gross margin collapsing to 20.3%, and 2026 growth guidance relying heavily on acquisitions and government contracts rather than proven operating leverage.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Avoid until Ask Sage and CargoSeer convert into real growth, because the core business is still shrinking sharply and the AI narrative is masking weak margins, negative cash flow, and dilution risk.
